objective: Test whether coverage-gated credit is a useful adaptive bridge between group-relative and critic-style updates in closed-loop toy training.
hypothesis: When critic replay coverage is deliberately limited, falling back to group-relative credit for undercovered states should improve final return over a pure group-total update without relying on critic TD everywhere.
method: Run the tabular closed-loop toy training benchmark with fixed seeds, fixed generated-token budget, low critic replay limit, and matched policy update settings for group_total, group_length_norm, critic_td, and coverage_gated.

data_boundaries.train: Toy policy-gradient rollouts from seeds 11, 29, 47, 83, 131, 173, 211, 257, 307, and 359.
data_boundaries.validation: Aggregate final return and success from the same pre-registered low-coverage toy setting; no hyperparameter search after seeing heldout rows.
data_boundaries.heldout: Replay the same command with a fresh ledger entry before calling the result verified.
